Agile Kookaburras... ATO Payday Super... $5M') is GONE. Refine on both letters equally PRIYA-grounded, no leak (verify-twice). Studio UI corroboration: 0 console errors, 0 operator PII.

Account B internal read-only analytics degrade honestly: GET /jobs/{id}/insights -> 200 needsResume=True, scored=False, matchedSkills=[], skillsMatched=0, overall=0 — resume-derived dims zeroed, no operator content [VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE]. The no-resume cover-letter-insights needsResume=True branch is naturally unreachable in prod (a no-resume user cannot possess a letter) — it is the SAME resolve_user_resume_text(allow_operator_fallback=False) code path as the job-insights empty-state proven here [INFERRED for that sub-branch only].

Account B POST /agents/fit-scorer/run -> HTTP 422 'Add your resume before scoring jobs against it.' with NO CHARGE: DB UsageQuota runsUsed 0->0 and spendUsedUsd unchanged across the call; the seeded job's fitScore stays NULL (no operator-derived score persisted). No operator PII in the 422 body. Cross-checked quota before+after (DB) and job-list fitScore (API).

PARTIAL fix, NOT fully closed. The fix's skill-prioritization works: for a messy JD the panel now surfaces real skills FIRST (gRPC,Kafka,Rust,reliability,distributed) so real skills are no longer crowded out past the 10-item cap (that sub-defect IS fixed). BUT the tokenizer-artifact class the finding is literally about STILL surfaces: a messy JD yields keyword chips 'EngineerLocation','AustraliaApply','ResponsibilitiesBuild','RequirementsExperience','EmployerShare'. Direct fresh test of the pure _jd_keywords() on the EXACT original-finding pattern ('Project ManagerLocation: Toronto, CanadaPosted...') returns 'ManagerLocation' as the #1 keyword chip — the identical token the original finding cited. Root cause: _is_semantic_keyword does not detect CamelCase word-concatenation artifacts, and _skill_score REWARDS them (+45 for internal uppercase, treating 'EngineerLocation' like 'PostgreSQL'). Filed as new finding NF-final-PII-001. NOTE: this is a cosmetic coverage-panel quality gap, NOT a PII leak — the PII-leak class is unaffected.

Applications tracker subtitle (data-testid=tracker-subtitle) renders LIVE as '6 pipeline items across 8 stages' — the 'N pipeline item(s)' label, NOT 'N active applications'. Captured from the real UI (Playwright, fresh session, 0 console errors). Frontend source confirms `{activeCount} pipeline item{activeCount===1?'':'s'} across 8 stages` (applications/page.tsx:328).

A no-op tailor (PRIYA resume vs a Pastry-Chef mismatch job, async worker) resolved to BackgroundJob status='completed' (NOT 'failed'), result.noChangesApplied=true, changes=0, with an INFORMATIONAL message 'No verifiable changes could be applied — every suggested edit was unsupported by your evidence, so your resume is unchanged and you were not charged.' DB confirms the AgentRun row status='completed', error=NULL, costUsd=0.0000 (NOT failed, refunded). No leaked exception-class text on the no-op path (contrast the no-resume MissingResumeError path — see NF-final-PII-002).

new_findings.summary: The JD Keyword Coverage panel still surfaces CamelCase word-concatenation scraper artifacts as 'keywords' (e.g. 'ManagerLocation', 'EngineerLocation', 'ResponsibilitiesBuild', 'RequirementsExperience', 'EmployerShare'). The MV-cover-letter-studio-006 fix added real-skill prioritization (real skills now rank first and are not crowded out past the 10-item cap) but did not filter this artifact class — and _skill_score actively rewards internal-uppercase tokens (+45), so a glued token like 'EngineerLocation' scores like 'PostgreSQL'.
new_findings.repro: GET /cover-letters/{id}/insights for a letter whose job JD has scraper-glued words; OR call app.routers.cover_letters._jd_keywords('Project ManagerLocation: Toronto, CanadaPosted 3 days ago...') -> ['ManagerLocation','CanadaPosted','agoAbout','Project',...]. Fix suggestion: split/penalize interior-caps tokens that are not known acronyms/skills in _is_semantic_keyword, or split on lower->UPPER boundaries before scoring.
new_findings.detail: MEDIUM severity — degrades a paid intelligence-rail feature by telling the user their letter is 'missing' nonsense keywords; NOT a PII leak.

new_findings.summary: With AETHER_ASYNC_GENERATION=true (prod), a no-resume user's cover-letter and tailor runs return 202 then resolve to a BackgroundJob status='failed' whose user-visible error carries the internal exception-class prefix: 'MissingResumeError: Add your resume before generating a cover letter.' The synchronous paths (email/refine/fit-scorer) instead return a clean 422 'Add your resume before ...' with NO class-name prefix, and the NoChangesApplied no-op path is gracefully marked 'completed'. So the benign 'add your resume' condition is presented as a red 'failed' job with a leaked class name only on the async outbound paths.
new_findings.repro: As a no-resume user with async on: POST /agents/cover-letter/run {job_id} -> 202; poll GET /agents/jobs/{id} -> status='failed', error='MissingResumeError: Add your resume before generating a cover letter.' Root: worker tasks.py catches MissingResumeError under the generic `except Exception` -> mark_failed + _honest_message (which prefixes non-HTTPException with the class name). Fix suggestion: handle MissingResumeError like NoChangesApplied (complete with an informational needs-resume result, or strip the class-name prefix). Acceptance impact: honest + PII-free + not a 5xx, so it does NOT reopen the PII class — but it diverges from the brief's expected clean '422 Add your resume' on the async paths.
